SUMMARY
This impressive three-bedroom home offers generous living space, thoughtful layout, and beautifully maintained gardens, making it an ideal choice for families or anyone seeking room to grow.


DESCRIPTION
Step through the front door into a bright and spacious hallway, offering plenty of room for shoes and coats, and giving an immediate sense of welcome.

Just off the hall is a convenient downstairs WC.

The large lounge sits to the front of the property, featuring a wide window that fills the room with natural light. This inviting space flows seamlessly into the generous dining room, where a sliding door opens directly onto the rear garden — perfect for indoor-outdoor living and entertaining.

From the dining room, enter the stylish kitchen, fitted with modern white base and wall units, contrasting worktops, and a large window overlooking the garden. A door leads through to the useful utility room, offering space for a washing machine and additional storage. Beyond this is a lean-to porch, providing even more practical storage options.



Upstairs, the property boasts two spacious double bedrooms and a well-proportioned single bedroom. The master bedroom features built-in wardrobes, and the first floor is completed by a family bathroom.



Outside, the rear garden is a true highlight — an impressive, well-established space with a patio area, lawned sections, mature shrubs, and beautiful fruit trees, creating a peaceful and private outdoor retreat.

To the front, you’ll find a gravelled garden and driveway with parking for two cars, leading up to the garage.

This is a wonderfully spacious home with excellent storage, generous rooms, and charming gardens.

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
SUMMARY
Don't miss the opportunity to own this elegant and versatile home in one of Somerset's most sought-after towns. A beautifully presented three-storey Victorian red brick terraced property offering a blend of period character and stylish contemporary living.


DESCRIPTION
Welcome to this beautifully presented Victorian red brick terraced home offering spacious, stylish living across three floors This light-filled 3-bedroom property combines timeless character with a contemporary twist, featuring open-plan living accommodation that flows effortlessly through the living, dining, and kitchen areas. The interiors are modern and tastefully styled, creating a warm and inviting atmosphere throughout. Outside the property enjoys a lovely mature private garden with the benefit of a separate garden studio - ideal as a creative space for artists or remote working - and a garage provides secure off-street parking or additional storage.

Situated on the edge of town, this home benefits from a peaceful setting while still being within easy reach of Frome's amenities, shops, schools, and transport links - making it an ideal choice for families - so viewings are highly recommended!

Entrance Hall 
Welcoming entrance hall featuring a wooden door part glazed to allow in natural light. Wooden flooring adds warmth and brightness. A traditional radiator and a staircase leads to the first floor landing. Doorway into the main living accommodation.

Living Dining Room 
A light and spacious living room featuring impressive high ceilings that enhance the sense of openness and airiness. A striking stone surround fireplace serves as a beautiful focal point, adding warmth and character to the space. The room benefits from a large double-glazed window to the front aspect, allowing for plenty of natural light throughout the day but also fitted with white wooden shutters for shade if required. Elegant wooden flooring runs underfoot which extends to the rest of the ground floor accommodation, complementing the traditional-style radiator and seamlessly continuing into the adjoining dining room, creating an inviting and cohesive living area. The dining room area featuring a nearly floor-to-ceiling double-glazed window that floods the space with natural light and offers uninterrupted views over the rear garden. A charming fireplace serves as a focal point, adding warmth and character to the room. Beneath the staircase, a range of built-in storage cupboards provide practical and discreet storage solutions. The room flows seamlessly into the adjoining kitchen, creating an open and versatile space ideal for both everyday living and entertaining.

Kitchen 
A sleek and contemporary, generously sized kitchen forms part of a stylish extension. Midnight blue base cabinets create a bold contrast, complemented by warm wooden worktops. A one-and-a-half black composite sink is seamlessly inset into the counter, adding both functionality and elegance. The kitchen is fully fitted with integral appliances, including a double electric oven, electric hob, and dishwasher, all chosen to blend effortlessly with the modern aesthetic.

Expansive aluminium-framed glazed doors flood the space with natural light and open directly into the garden, leading onto a beautifully crafted wooden terrace-perfect for indoor-outdoor living and entertaining.

First Floor Landing 
Balustrade landing with access into the loft ad return staircase rising to the second floor with built in storage cupboard under. Traditional stripped pine doors opening into all rooms. Wooden flooring which extends throughout the first floor accommodation. Built in airing cupboard.

Bedroom Two 
This generously sized double bedroom features two double-glazed sash windows to the front aspect, allowing for plenty of natural light and a bright, airy feel. A charming exposed and painted brick fireplace adds character and warmth to the space, while a sleek column radiator offers efficient and stylish heating.

Bedroom Three 
This charming third bedroom boasts generous proportions and a tranquil ambiance. Double glazed sash window to the rear aspect frames elevated views over tree tops towards the adjacent nature reserve. An attractive painted cat iron fireplace adds character and period charm.

Bathroom 
An excellent-sized bathroom featuring a traditional-style modern suite. The space includes a full-sized bath with a shower attachment, a separate shower cubicle for added convenience, and a classic wash hand basin with WC. A double-glazed sash window to the rear aspect provides natural light while maintaining privacy. Heated towel.

Second Floor 

Main Bedroom 
A generously sized attic room offering excellent proportions and versatility. The space features sloping ceilings with impressive head height throughout, creating an airy and comfortable atmosphere. A charming gabled, double-glazed window to the rear aspect frames delightful tree-top views while allowing plenty of natural light to flood the room. Finished with a stylish column radiator and ample floor space.

Outside 

Garden 
A generously sized rear garden which is fully enclosed and well stocked with a variety of plants, trees and shrubs. There is raised decked seating area abutting the rear of the property which then leads down to a large long lawned area.

Garage & Parking 
Single garage located to the rear of the garden. This is access via a shared track nearby that gives rear access to several properties on Rodden Road. There is off road parking to the front of the property as well as on street parking.

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
SUMMARY
A stunning individual property which offers an abundance of light and space with stunning views to the rear over the large garden towards the Longleat estate. Only an internal viewing will allow potential purchasers to truly appreciate the standard of the finish, and enjoy the views on offer.


DESCRIPTION
This wonderful barn conversion lies between the towns of Frome and Warminster and is situated within the county of Somerset. It is set within a small development of former farm buildings that are believed to have once been stables and accommodation associated with the Longleat Estate. The property currently consists of a lovely living room with wood burning stove, kitchen / breakfast room with doors leading to the garden, double bedroom and stunning wet room. On the second floor you will find the master bedroom with en-suite bathroom, and from here can enjoy the most fantastic far-reaching views towards Longleat. To the rear of the property there is a good sized well maintained garden with lawned areas and a variety of flower beds, a small paddock, plus a detached studio / home office with power supply. Viewing comes highly recommended.

Kitchen / Diner 14' 6" x 12' ( 4.42m x 3.66m )
Wooden door giving entrance into the kitchen. You are immediately met with lovely views to the rear through the rear doors into the garden. Wooden floors run through the room with polished steel base units and solid wood worktops. Built in oven with electric hob above. Double bowl sink built into worktop. There is an integrated dishwasher and washing machine as well as space for a fridge freezer. There is also plenty of space for a good-sized dining table and chairs.

Living Room 14' x 13' 7" ( 4.27m x 4.14m )
Glazed door to front and rear aspect window with feature deep sill. Quarry tiled flooring with a beautiful multi-fuel burner in the corner of the room. Electric storage heater.

Bedroom Two 14' x 12' 4" ( 4.27m x 3.76m )
Two rear aspect Velux windows. Night storage heater.

Inner Hall 
Stairs rising to the first floor. Understairs storage area. Door to:

Shower Room 
Rear aspect stained glass window. Tiled floor and part tiled walls. Suite comprising walkin shower, vanity wash hand basin, and low level W.C. There is also underfloor heating.

Principle Bedroom  14' x 12' 2" ( 4.27m x 3.71m )
Glazed window to rear with glorious views to the rear over the garden. Exposed end-grained wooden flooring with 'original' wood panelled walls. There is a door to outside stairs which give access to the front of the property. Opens into:

Ensuite Bathroom 
Side aspect Velux window. A built-in panelled bath with tiled splash back. A low level W.C. Wash hand basin. Built in eaves storage space.

Outside 

Front 
A shared driveway leads to an allocated gravelled area to the front of the property where there are mature shrubs, climbers and trellis, and allocated space for two cars. There are stairs rising to the first floor which lead into the master bedroom. The property can also be entered via the kitchen and living room.

Rear Garden & Paddock 
Large landscaped rear garden which offers a high level of privacy along with very enviable views across the garden and towards the Longleat estate. Steps, which offer a storage area beneath them, lead down to the garden from the kitchen.
There is a block paved pathway with areas of lawn to each side with mature raised flower beds which winds through the garden. There are a mixture of vegetable plots and fruit trees which can be found as well as a green house.
At the head of the garden there is a substantial paddock area which is fully enclosed. This once used to be a large vegetable plot but could now be used for a variety of reasons.

Detached Studio 
There is a detached studio/office with power and lighting as well as potential plumbing as there is a tap located near by. It is approximately 24ft in length and could be used for a range of uses including studio, office, or extra accommodation.


DIRECTIONS
From our Frome office head through the Market Place then turn left onto the A362. Continue on the A362 through two sets of traffic lights. At Wallbridge roundabout take the 1st exit onto the A362. At T-junction turn left onto the A362 and continue for about and the turn in for Woodcock House will be about about a mile down the road on the right hand side.

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
Set slightly back from the street behind a charming, walled front garden, the house immediately makes a strong first impression. The attractive local stone façade is softened by delicate trellis detailing, ideal for climbing plants, giving the property a quietly elegant presence in the very centre of Rode’s thriving village life. Inside, the tone is set by a beautifully composed interior that feels both characterful and confidently modern. The sitting room is warm and inviting, anchored by an original exposed beam and a wood-burning stove set on a slate hearth. Engineered wooden flooring runs underfoot, while contemporary light fittings, softly washed paint tones and traditional-style iron radiators with exposed copper pipework create a carefully balanced aesthetic. Thoughtful details such as a recessed niche and useful storage alcove add both charm and practicality, and the staircase rises naturally from this space to the upper floors.

To the rear, the open-plan kitchen and dining area has been intelligently redesigned and is flooded with natural light from south-east facing windows. The dining space features a slate tiled floor, industrial-style pendant lighting and a sleek, modern radiator, providing a subtle contrast to the period elements found elsewhere. The kitchen itself is finished to a high standard, with granite worktops, integrated appliances and a substantial larder/utility cupboard, offering excellent storage without compromising on style. A glazed door open directly onto the private courtyard garden, creating an easy connection between indoor and outdoor living.

The first floor hosts two well-proportioned double bedrooms, both finished in crisp white tones that enhance the sense of space and light, while exposed feature beams add character and warmth. The master bedroom has pale blue feature paint to either end. One bedroom enjoys direct access to a decked roof terrace, a rare and appealing retreat. Also on this level is the beautifully appointed family bathroom, featuring a freestanding bath, walk-in shower and generous built-in storage. Bathed in natural light from two large skylights, the room feels airy, calm and luxurious. The second floor offers a versatile third double bedroom, again finished in clean white with character beams, complemented by ample built-in storage, a wardrobe on the landing and a separate cloakroom with basin. This flexible space works equally well as a guest suite, home office or creative studio.

Externally, the property enjoys a secluded and low-maintenance rear courtyard garden, alongside the elevated roof terrace. An allocated parking space is located conveniently to the rear of the house.

Offered with no onward chain, this is a thoughtfully curated home that blends period integrity with modern design, set within the conservation area of one of the area’s most picturesque and sought-after village locations.

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
Set on the edge of the ever-desirable village of Rode forming part of an exclusive development, ideally positioned for direct links to the popular historic city of Bath, is this beautifully presented modern three-bedroom family home.

The front door opens into a large, light and airy entrance hall which is a great first impression of the home.

The modern kitchen comprises a range of base and wall mounted cabinets, beautiful worktops, a sink inset into the worktop, an integrated oven and fitted hob complete with stainless steel extractor hood overhead. There is also an integrated fridge/freezer, dishwasher and washing machine. The room offers plenty of space for a dining table and chairs with double doors leading to the entrance hallway and double doors opening onto the garden patio. There are two kitchen upgrades that have been installed (after purchase, but from the original installer), these are the island with additional storage plus the extended oak topped cupboards and wine rack seen to the right of the integrated fridge/freezer.

The dual aspect lounge is a tranquil, light and airy room with ample space for lounge furnishings, also enjoying double doors opening onto the patio. 

The ground floor is completed with a modern fitted cloakroom and under-stair storage cupboard.

Stairs lead to the first floor and onto a spacious landing.

On the first floor there is a well-proportioned master bedroom with a stunning high specification modern en-suite three-piece shower room. There is a secondary light and airy double bedroom and good size third double bedroom, both of which are served by the modern three-piece family bathroom comprising of a fitted bath with shower over, wall mounted vanity unit and W.C. There are also custom made and installed wardrobes on the stairs landing recess.

OUTSIDE

Externally, the home offers a highly private fence enclosed low maintenance landscaped garden. Adjoining the house there is a patio/seating area with a decked seating area and level lawn beyond. There is also a timber shed providing plenty of useful storage. To the front of the house there is designated parking for two vehicles. An EV charger has been installed in the side entrance.

AGENT’S NOTE

The development has private visitor parking. There is a maintenance cost paid a for general upkeep and maintenance of communal areas.

LOCATION

Rode is one of the area's most popular and sought-after villages, with a green at its centre. It is within commuting distance of both Bath and Bristol and offers easy access to the adjoining towns of Frome, Bradford on Avon and Trowbridge, while Westbury has a regular fast connection to London Paddington. Village amenities include pubs, a very popular primary school, post office/general store with fantastic café, village hall and playing fields. Private schools are to be found in Bath and Warminster.

Quote ref EB0191 Dating back to circa 1660, this beautifully presented Grade II listed cottage is full of warmth, history and character, having once served as a village inn. Thoughtfully maintained, it seamlessly blends period charm with a welcoming, homely feel throughout.

Set in the sought-after village of Maiden Bradley, this is a rare opportunity to own a truly special piece of heritage. Returning to the market after generating strong previous interest, this is an opportunity not to be missed.

This delightful Grade 2 listed cottage over three floors offers plenty of space for you to create the home of your dreams, it is exceptionally maintained and ready to move into.

You will be delighted by the bucolic feel of this period home, thoughtfully decorated throughout but retaining the authenticity of its origins. The living room is spacious but cosy, adorned with wooden beams. The focal point of the room, the inglenook fire with striking Bressummer Beam and a wood burner, adds not only warmth but also a touch of rustic charm to the space creating a warm and inviting atmosphere. Imagine curling up on a cold winter's night with an enjoyable book and a cup of tea, the crackling fire casting a soft glow around you.

There is a storage cupboard providing ample space to keep your belongings and small household appliances organised and out of sight.

The kitchen in this cottage is a true delight for anyone who loves to cook. Featuring shaker style cabinetry with a complimenting solid wood worktop and Belfast sink. The kitchen is equipped with a Hotpoint electric cooker* making meal preparation a breeze. There is space for a fridge freezer and dishwasher and room for a breakfast table. French Doors lead out into the garden.

The dining room provides plenty of room for a large table and additional furniture where you can gather with family and friends for meals and celebrations. French doors lead out into the garden.

A downstairs cloakroom adds convenience for both residents and guests.

There is also a utility room which is perfect for all your shoes and accessories, ensuring a clutter free environment. There are also spaces for a washing machine and tumble dryer. The oil-fired boiler is also located here.

One of the rare features of this home is the original cellar which is used purposefully to store wine.

On the first floor, the cottage features two double bedrooms, each offering a serene retreat for rest and relaxation. The second bedroom is light filled, ideal for a growing family or hosting guests, providing a built-in cupboard, there is a delightful ornate fireplace adding a unique touch to the space.

The third bedroom, whilst used as office space, has room for a double bed and furniture and benefits from built in shelving and wardrobe.

The beautifully appointed family bathroom is a true sanctuary, recently refurbished there is a shower over the bath, vanity sink and toilet, this bathroom caters to your every need.

On the second floor is the main bedroom complete with built-in cupboards in a dressing area, providing sufficient storage space and a clutter free environment while maintaining the vintage aesthetic of the cottage.  There is also a recently refurbished en-suite. Featuring a corner shower cubicle, vanity unit with sink, a back to wall toilet and an infrared radiator*. The bedroom itself is spacious with room for a king or superior king size bed and additional furniture.

The garden is a true oasis, featuring a patio area where you can relax and unwind, surrounded by lush shrubs and foliage whilst the boundary treatments provide ample privacy. Imagine sipping your morning coffee in this serene setting or hosting intimate gatherings with loved ones in this delightful outdoor space.

In addition to the charming cottage and stunning garden, this property also benefits from off road driveway parking which has provision for an electric car charger, there is also a timber shed for gardening accessories, a discreet bin storage area. The current owners also have an allotment plot at the Community Gardens which is available by separate negotiation.

In conclusion, the cottage's historical charm and unique features make it a desirable property for those seeking a traditional home. Whether you are looking for a weekend retreat or a permanent residence, this property offers the perfect blend of old-world charm and contemporary living. Come and get your own piece of vintage.

Why choose Maiden Bradley?

Maiden Bradley is a picturesque village located in the beautiful countryside of Wiltshire. Known for its charming cottages and historic buildings, Maiden Bradley offers a tranquil setting for residents and visitors alike. The village's rich history and stunning landscapes make it a popular destination for those seeking a peaceful retreat in the English countryside. Living in Maiden Bradley offers a quaint countryside experience with the Bradley Hare Pub set within the Duchy of Somerset estate and the recently opened family run Bradley Pantry adding to the charm. The refurbished pub provides a cosy place to unwind and socialise, while the store offers locally sourced produce, and you can even sit down and enjoy coffee and fabulous homemade delights. There are also community gardens currently with around thirty members with new members welcomed allowing you to get involved in the growing of vegetables, flowers and soft fruit for the residents and their families. The community garden also has hives and wildlife areas for maximum biodiversity. A village hall with regular events and a recreation ground. The Potting Shed, which describes itself as a private and secluded boutique beauty studio. These local establishments create a sense of community and make daily life more enjoyable for residents in Maiden Bradley to an already close-knit community.

Do not miss the opportunity to make this charming cottage your new home. Whether you are a first-time buyer, a growing family, or looking to downsize, this property ticks all the boxes. So, to delve deeper into the details of this fantastic offering, book your personal tour with Emma Butcher today where you will have the opportunity to see it for yourself and learn more about this lovely home and its potential.

The property also has the additional benefits of a Grant boiler, oil fired central heating, double-glazed windows, eaves storage in the main bedroom, outside tap, Shed, off street parking for several cars.

Kindly note some items mentioned* or seen in the photographs may not be included in the property sale or could be available by separate negotiation. Please check with the Property Agent. For further information or details about this property please visit. emmabutcher.exp.uk.com

An immaculately presented three bedroom period home situated in the picturesque village of Hinton Charterhouse. The house boasts well balanced, light and airy flexible accommodation set over three floors along with generous gardens and garaging.

Description - As you enter the property, you are welcomed into a bright and spacious dual-aspect reception room. This open-plan living area is filled with natural light and offers a warm, inviting atmosphere, enhanced by a charming log burner and useful built-in storage. The space flows seamlessly through to a modern, well-appointed kitchen, which features a gas hob, a traditional Belfast sink, and generous cupboard space for storage. From here, a characterful stable-style door opens out onto the patio, providing easy access to the garden and creating a lovely connection between the indoor and outdoor living spaces.

The first floor hosts two well-proportioned bedrooms, both offering comfortable and versatile accommodation, along with a stylish family bathroom fitted with a four-piece suite. Occupying the entire second floor is a further bedroom, which benefits from its own en-suite bathroom.

Externally, the property enjoys on-street parking to the front. To the rear, there is a delightful patio area that is ideal for outdoor seating and entertaining, leading onto a generous lawned garden that provides plenty of space to relax and enjoy the outdoors through the warmer months. At the end of the garden there is a fantastic garage that could be converted into a home office if required or be kept for storage or as a workshop. There is also further parking here.

Location - Hinton Charterhouse is an attractive village in Somerset located approximately five miles south of Bath. The village offers a range of local amenities including a Post Office, public houses and a well established cricket club, with a nearby farm shop providing additional local produce and conveniences. Well-regarded primary schools can also be found in the neighbouring villages of Norton St Philip and Freshford.

The nearby city of Bath provides a wide variety of shopping, restaurants, cafés and cultural attractions. Notable facilities include the Theatre Royal Bath and the Thermae Bath Spa, along with extensive sporting facilities at University of Bath. Bath also offers excellent transport links, including a regular bus service from the village into the city centre. The M4 motorway (Junction 18) lies around ten miles to the north, while Bath Spa railway station provides mainline services to London Paddington, Swindon, Bristol and South Wales.

The property is also conveniently located for access to a number of well-regarded schools including Beechen Cliff School, Prior Park College, King Edward's School and Monkton Combe.

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
An attractive village centre house offered with no onward chain. With three double bedrooms, two reception rooms, a low maintenance courtyard garden and a wealth of period features, this property is perfect for both residents and investors.

3 Frome Road is an immaculate home, having been recently refurbished to a very high standard. It offers neutral carpeting throughout, cast iron radiators, designer sanitary ware and modern fixtures and fittings throughout.

You enter the home via a well-lit porch, which leads directly into the comfortable lounge, with dual aspect views and log burning stove, providing ample space for family gatherings. There is also a dining area, with room for a family sized table and chairs, with large niche and under stairs storage. From here you can access the generous, fully-equipped kitchen, with high-end appliances, including a single AGA. There is a small breakfast bar, illuminated by a skylight, and a small WC. There is also a door out to the quaint and sunny courtyard, perfect for entertaining. 

On the first floor the master bedroom is currently furnished with a king-sized bed, and has a small built-in cupboard. There is another, more compact double and the family bathroom, with marble tiling, free standing bath and brushed gold brassware. 

On the second floor there is a further bedroom in the eaves, with en-suite wet room.

Outside there is a south facing raised garden and easy to manage additional courtyard with views out to the village. To the front of the property is a small secure enclosed area that houses the bin and log stores, with plenty of room for bikes. Potential for parking a small car if adapted. 

The thriving village of Beckington offers a lively Anglican church, two public houses, the Mes Amis café/deli, tennis, football and cricket clubs, village hall, village school and Springmead private day nursery and prep school. Recreational facilities locally are good with Babington House, Orchardleigh Golf club, fishing, shooting and beautiful open countryside all on the doorstep. The excellent farmers' co-operative, Mole Valley Farmers, where you can purchase a huge selection of goods and the award winning Whiterow Farm Shop, with a first-class butchery, fishmonger, bakery, and delicatessen are both nearby. The historic market town of Frome is four miles away and features the highest number of listed buildings in Somerset. Longleat House and safari park and its many attractions are nearby. Private schools are to be found in Bath, Bruton, Castle Cary, Cranmore, Glastonbury, Street, Warminster, and Wells.

SUMMARY
Charming three-bedroom terrace in a quiet cul-de-sac, featuring a bright living room, stylish open-plan kitchen/dining with log burner, two doubles (one with Juliette balcony), modern bathroom, parking for three, and a rear garden with patio and lawn enjoying stunning views.


DESCRIPTION
Tucked away in a quiet cul-de-sac, this delightful three-bedroom terrace house offers stylish living spaces and breathtaking views.

Step inside to a welcoming living room, ideal for relaxing at the end of the day. Along the hallway is the heart of the home – a wonderful open-plan kitchen, dining and family area. The kitchen is beautifully designed, featuring a central island with integrated hob and cooker, combining practicality with elegance.

The dining area is a highlight, with its charming stone fireplace and log burner creating a cosy focal point. From here, sliding doors open onto the rear garden, framing stunning views and filling the space with natural light. A handy utility room provides space for a washing machine, with a sink and access to the garden, while a convenient WC with sink completes the ground floor. Throughout, the home is finished with lovely flooring that enhances the flow.

Upstairs, the family bathroom is well-appointed with a corner bath, separate shower cubicle and heated towel rail. Including two generous double bedrooms and a single. One of the doubles enjoys French doors opening to a Juliette balcony, offering spectacular views, while the master bedroom is spacious and bright, with two front-facing windows.

Outside to the front, a gravelled driveway provides parking for up to three cars. To the rear, the garden features a patio area perfect for entertaining leading down to a grassed lawn where you can soak in the beautiful open views.

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
Located on the edge of the popular village of Rode, this well-proportioned 3 bedroom detached home is presented in excellent condition throughout and is offered to the market with no onward chain. The property enjoys a pleasing balance of modern accommodation and a rural outlook.

The accommodation is arranged over two floors. On the ground floor, an entrance hall provides access to a cloak cupboard, which was formerly a WC and offers potential for reinstatement. The spacious living room features a fireplace and built-in storage, while the modern, well-equipped kitchen opens into a generous dining area. This, in turn, leads to a bright garden room with direct access to the rear garden, creating a practical and flexible living space.

The first floor offers three good-sized bedrooms, with the principle bedroom benefiting from an en suite shower room and attractive views across open fields. A family bathroom and an airing cupboard complete the accommodation on this level.

Externally, the property features a driveway providing off-street parking, a single garage, and gated side access to the rear garden. The private garden is predominantly laid to lawn and complemented by a decked area, ideal for outdoor dining and entertaining.

4 Churchfields is situated in the popular Somerset village of Rode.  The village benefits from Rode First School, a Post Office/village store, 2 public houses (The Cross Keys and The Mill at Rode) and St Lawrence Church. The surrounding countryside provides excellent walks including meandering strolls along the River Frome.

The village is also well positioned for access to surrounding towns, which include Trowbridge (approx. 4 miles), Bradford on Avon (approx. 5 miles), Frome (approx. 5 miles) and Warminster (approx. 8 miles). Amenities including retail outlets, pubs and restaurants, cinema and sporting facilities can be found within these towns along with rail links.

The World Heritage City of Bath (approx. 10 miles) provides a complete range of retail outlets together with many other amenities including The Theatre Royal, a number of fine restaurants and excellent sporting facilities. There is also a mainline railway station providing direct access to London Paddington, Bristol and south Wales.

The M4 motorway junctions 17 and 18 are both approximately 20 miles and access to the M3 via the A303 is also easily reached.

8 Frome Road is a charming Grade II listed house set in over half an acre of grounds that now has full planning permission approved to create a stunning three bedroom family size house with a separate garden annex. Planning reference: 2024/1979/HSE

Currently a shell, our vendor has carried out extensive works over the last two years to include a new slate roof, new chimneys and new floors. 

The proposed accommodation on the ground floor includes a large study, a snug, an impressive open planned kitchen/dining/living space with two sets of doors spilling onto the gardens and a utility with w.c.

The proposed accommodation for the first floor includes Three bedrooms. The master is a well proportioned and double aspect room with an en-suite, a dressing room and fabulous views over the gardens and the open fields beyond. The two further bedrooms are a good size and share a 'Jack & Jill' style en suite.

Outside

The majority of the garden sits to the side, front and rear of the house and is enclosed by mature hedges and currently laid mainly to lawn with some mature trees. It provides a wonderful back drop to the house and even if the footprint was extended there is still ample space to enjoy.

As part of the planning approved, there is a garden office with annex attached.

Agents note 

Once the extension the planning has approved is completed and the house and grounds are renovated, we would anticipate an end value of £850,000 - £950,000, depending on the spec of finish, as of March 2026.

Services

We are informed by our vendor that mains water and private drainage are connected, our vendor has indicated that the drainage may need to be replaced. The heating system used to run off oil. Potential purchasers must rely on their own enquiries with regards to the location of any services and making any connection into them.

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
Why you'll like it
Set within a quiet and highly sought after cul de sac on the ever popular Leighton Green development, this spacious three double bedroom detached family home enjoys a particularly enviable position. The property is tucked away with no passing traffic, creating a peaceful setting, while still being within easy reach of local amenities. Open fields and countryside walks are close by, making the location ideal for dog owners and those who enjoy a quieter, outdoor lifestyle.

You enter the property into an impressive and welcoming entrance hall, which immediately gives a sense of space. From here there is access to both the kitchen dining room and the living room, along with three generous storage cupboards and stairs rising to the first floor.

The living room is positioned at the rear of the property and provides an excellent space for relaxing or entertaining. A feature fireplace creates a focal point, while a large window overlooks the rear garden, allowing plenty of natural light to flow through. A doorway leads into the dining room, which offers ample space for a family sized dining table and chairs. Sliding patio doors and a further rear door open directly onto the garden, helping to connect the indoor and outdoor space during the warmer months.

The dining room leads through to the utility room, which in turn provides access to the downstairs WC and a further doorway into the kitchen dining room at the front of the property. The kitchen is fitted with modern wall and base units and includes an integrated oven and hob, fridge freezer, and integrated dishwasher. There is also space for a breakfast table and chairs, making this a practical and sociable area for everyday use.

On the first floor, the landing is bright and spacious, with a window allowing natural light to fill the space. From here there is access to all three bedrooms and the family bathroom. All three bedrooms are well proportioned double rooms and benefit from fitted wardrobes. They are served by the family bathroom, which comprises a bath with overhead shower, wash hand basin, and WC.

Externally, the property continues to impress. The rear garden is fully enclosed and generous in size, mainly laid to lawn with a selection of mature shrubs and plants. A patio seating area provides an ideal spot for relaxing or entertaining, while a side door gives direct access into the double garage. The garage measures over 28ft in length and benefits from double doors to the front.

To the front of the property, a spacious block paved driveway provides parking for multiple vehicles, along with access to the double garage via an up and over door.

Internal viewing is highly recommended to fully appreciate the space, privacy, and particularly desirable position this family home enjoys on Leighton Green. 

Property location
Westbury is a small market town situated at the western edge of Wiltshire, besides the chalk downlands that form Salisbury Plain. The most famous landmark of the area is the vastly impressive Hillside White Horse carving, which is both the oldest and largest White Horse in Wiltshire. The town offers a range of shopping and leisure facilities including, library, sports and leisure centre, schools, churches, doctors, dentist surgeries, three supermarkets, post office and the oldest swimming pool in the country. We also have a varied selection of coffee shops, restaurants, public houses and a popular wine bar. Westbury is exceptionally well positioned for all major road and railway connections with the mainline railway station offering links to London (1 hour 26 minutes), Wales and the whole of the South West of England.

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
A superbly presented three-bedroom, three-storey stone cottage, thoughtfully refurbished to a high standard and quietly positioned behind a charming low stone boundary with a private approach, set within the sought-after and historic Keyford area of Frome. The property enjoys a beautifully established and well-tended rear garden, providing a delightful outdoor retreat, while a pedestrian right of way immediately outside the rear door creates a warm and neighbourly atmosphere, enhancing the cottage’s sense of community.

Internally, the accommodation is both light-filled and welcoming, centred around a cosy yet airy sitting room featuring a focal fireplace with log burner, offering ample space for comfortable furnishings and relaxed living. The stylish kitchen/breakfast room has been appointed with an excellent range of base and wall-mounted cabinetry, a bespoke sink set within quality worktops, integrated oven, fitted hob and further integrated appliances, all complemented by oak chevron flooring. The room retains a characterful original stone chimney with feature fireplace and provides space for a dining table and chairs, creating an ideal setting for everyday living and entertaining.

Arranged over the upper floors are three well-proportioned bedrooms, including a generous master bedroom to the front enjoying excellent natural light and space for bedroom furnishings. A further good-sized bedroom on the first floor is currently arranged as a home office, offering flexibility of use. The loft bedroom is a comfortable double with useful eaves storage. These rooms are served by a contemporary three-piece family bathroom comprising a fitted bath with overhead shower, wash hand basin and W.C.

Outside, the right of way beyond the rear door leads across the cottages to the mature landscaped garden, which is predominantly laid to lawn and enclosed by rustic wicca-style fencing, with well-stocked borders hosting a variety of established shrubs and planting. A pathway leads to a stone-built store, providing a highly useful workshop or outbuilding space.

LOCATION

Frome is a historic market town with many notable buildings and features the highest number of listed buildings in Somerset. Frome offers a range of shopping facilities, a sports centre, several cafés, a choice of pubs, local junior, middle and senior schools, several theatres and a cinema. Private schools are to be found in Wells, Bath, Warminster, Cranmore, Beckington, Glastonbury and Street. Bath and Bristol are within commuting distance, and the local railway station connects at Westbury for London, Paddington.

Tucked away in the popular Wallbridge area, this contemporary designed detached house offers well proportioned and naturally light accommodation, a low maintenance enclosed garden, parking and is offered with no onward chain.

Adjacent to the house is a driveway with comfortable parking for a large car. A gate from the driveway leads through the landscaped garden and to the house.

Internally the finish is of a high specification with excellent attention to detail. Coming to the market with guarantees in place, this is perfect for someone wanting a home with peace of mind, no work to do and high energy efficiency.

The front door leads into an entrance hall which leads through to the kitchen/breakfast room, the living room and Ash stairs rise to the first floor.

The kitchen includes an L shaped range of units with Quartz worktops, lots of storage and integrated appliances. There are dual aspect windows and plenty of space for a table and chairs.

The lounge is a great size with double sliding doors leading onto the gardens.

On the first floor is a large  and beautifully finished shower room and two bedrooms. The master is a good size double looking over the garden with a balcony off looking over the garden.

Air source heating. Underfloor on the ground floor. 

The garden is fully enclosed with good privacy, a patio seating area and room for a lawn beyond. There is garden lighting creating a lovely ambience for entertaining on a  summers evening.

The house is a short walk from the town centre and its many independent cafés and boutique shops, including Rye Bakery, Projects Frome, Moo and Two, Frome Hardware, Little Walcot and Frome Reclamation Yard. Frome’s popularity has surged in recent years; the town is routinely named one of the best places to live in the UK, thanks to its thriving cultural scene, historic architecture, and beautiful surrounding countryside. The Frome Independent, a monthly market showcasing local artisans and food producers, has helped put Frome on the map, attracting over 80,000 visitors annually.

Transport links are also very good. A mainline railway service runs direct services from Frome to London Paddington, with a journey time of around 86 minutes. Access to the national motorway network is via the A303 (M3) and Bristol Airport is less than 30 miles away.

A well-presented, three-bedroom detached home situated on the desirable east side of Frome, set within a quiet cul-de-sac and offering generous gardens along with excellent potential to extend (subject to the necessary planning permissions).

The property is approached via a spacious front garden, creating an attractive and private setting. Upon entering, the entrance hall provides ample space for coats and boots and includes a convenient downstairs WC.

To the rear of the property, the modern kitchen is well-appointed with an integrated Neff slide-and-hide oven, Neff warming drawer, dishwasher, American-style fridge/freezer, and space for both a washing machine and tumble dryer. Additional features include a breakfast bar, excellent storage, and direct access to the rear garden.

The remainder of the ground floor offers flexible living space. The sitting room is positioned at the front of the house, enjoying views over the garden and featuring a gas fire with stone surround. This flows through to the dining room, which comfortably accommodates a table for eight, and onward to the south-facing garden room, which enjoys pleasant views over the rear garden.

Upstairs, there are three well-proportioned double bedrooms and a newly fitted family shower room with a large walk-in shower and built-in storage. The main bedroom is located at the front of the property and benefits from fitted storage and a wardrobe. The remaining two bedrooms are both doubles, each with storage; the rear-facing bedroom enjoys views over the garden and is currently used as a study.

The garage offers further potential, with scope to extend over to create a fourth bedroom with en-suite (subject to planning). The property also benefits from a regularly serviced condenser boiler located in the garage and operated via a smart Hive control system.

The rear garden features a patio area with decked edging, leading to a large lawn bordered by mature shrubs, plants, and trees. A further seating area sits at the end of the garden, surrounded by trees. There are two sheds, a compost area, and side access to the garage, along with a side gate leading to the driveway. The driveway is accessed through a shared driveway with the neighbouring property and provides private parking for two vehicles. The front garden is mainly laid to lawn with established borders and mature trees.

There are many beautiful dog walks directly from the front door, including riverside paths, open countryside, and longer routes that could even take you as far as Longleat.

Frome is a historic market town located just under 13 miles from Bath and surrounded by beautiful countryside. The town offers a wide range of amenities, including good schools, two theatres, a cinema, and a railway station with direct links to Bath, Bristol, and London Paddington

POPULAR DEVELOPMENT


Book an appointment with our experienced Sales Negotiators at Keyford Meadows to find your new home with Curo. Location: Sandys Hill Lane, Frome, BA11 4DH. We are open 7 days a week, Monday: 12:30pm - 5:30pm and Tues - Sun: 10:00am - 5:30pm.

Plot 139-The Pensford is a brand new home located at our popular Keyford Meadows development. This high-quality three-bedroom home offers thoughtfully designed space to suit a variety of lifestyles and is built with sustainability in mind.
On the ground floor you will find a living room, an open plan kitchen/diner and a downstairs WC. On the first floor, the master bedroom boasts an en-suite shower room and fitted wardrobes. There are two further double bedrooms and a family bathroom with bath.
Externally, this new home comes with a single garage and driveway offering off-road parking for 2 cars, as well as an electric car charging unit. EPC rated B. Move in by summer 2026.

Keyford Meadows


Keyford Meadows is a development of one and two bedroom apartments and two, three, four and five bedroom houses. The perfect location for first-time buyers and families alike, Keyford Meadows straddles Sandys Hill Lane in Little Keyford, just on the outskirts of Frome. Homes are built to the highest standard at Keyford Meadows, with sustainability and the environment at the forefront. They will feature either highly efficient gas boilers or air source heat pumps. The design of the homes achieve a reduction in C02 emissions in excess of 10% of the recently updated Building Regulations. This is achieved by increased insulation, lower air tightness and the installation of waste water heat recovery to selected homes. Outside of the homes is an attractive environment that's ecologically rich and diverse with hectares of green space to be explored. Meanwhile, biodiversity in the area shall be greatly enhanced thanks to several features - such as integrated bat and bird boxes - adding to Frome's already wonderful ecosystem, aided by designated wildlife corridors that allow it to flourish.

*Curo*


At Curo we build quality, attractive homes designed for modern living, giving you confidence that you're making a great investment in your family's future. We believe a home is more than bricks and mortar; we create thoughtfully designed developments with connections, community and our customers at their heart. We're proud to be a business with social objectives.

That's why, instead of having shareholders, we re-invest all our profits to achieve our purpose - to create Homes for Good.

*Our dedication to you*


Curo is a registered developer with the New Homes Quality Board (NHQB), a non-profit organisation dedicated to improving the quality of new homes and customer service provided by developers. They set out requirements for us in terms of the quality of the homes we build, that we offer an inclusive, clear buying process and offer you, the customer, a quality service from start to finish.

New build homes; Greener and Cheaper


The average owner of a New build house is powered by 65% less energy, cutting energy bills by £165.00 a month. That's an annual saving up to £1,980* on household bills compared to pre-owned homeowners thanks to their in-use energy efficiency.

New build homes are increasingly more energy efficient. 86% of new build homes in the UK were rated an Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) of an A or B for energy efficiency. A stark contrast to less than 5% of existing homes that reach the same standard.

SmoothSale is delighted to present this rare and distinctive former chapel, thoughtfully transformed into a beautifully characterful home in the charming village of Wellow—just a short drive from Bath and only three miles from a Park & Ride offering direct access to the city.

Originally constructed in 1869 as the Ebenezer Chapel, this period end-of-terrace residence artfully combines historic architecture with contemporary comfort. Behind wrought iron gates, a path leads to the grand double entrance doors, opening into a layout rich with warmth and individuality.

The ground floor comprises a principal bedroom with en suite, a second single bedroom, and a separate family bathroom. Throughout the home, vibrant stained glass windows bathe the interiors in colour and natural light, creating a truly enchanting atmosphere.

Upstairs, oak stairs ascend to a modern galley kitchen, which flows through a graceful archway into a stunning open-plan living and dining area. With its feature fireplace and lofty ceilings, it's a perfect setting for both family life and entertaining.

The top floor hosts a third spacious double bedroom with en suite shower room and Velux windows, framing sweeping views of the surrounding countryside.

Outside, a peaceful courtyard garden offers a secluded spot for morning coffee or evening relaxation. Currently accessed via a shared path, there's potential (subject to consent) to create a direct connection from the kitchen for added convenience.

This unique home has served as both a cherished family residence and a successful Airbnb, making it ideal for flexible living or income generation. Wellow boasts a friendly, active community, a highly regarded primary school, local shop, and the popular Fox and Badger pub—all wrapped in stunning rural surroundings with superb transport links into Bath and beyond.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can secure this property with an optional reservation deposit of £2,500. This forms part of the property's total purchase price and is not an additional fee. The deposit draws up a legally binding exclusivity agreement which gives exclusive rights to purchase the property within an agreed timeframe.

The reservation deposit guarantees that the seller will remove their property from the market as soon as the offer is accepted and the deposit is paid. This means that the property is reserved for you, giving you time to complete the sale without the risk of being gazumped.

The fixed exclusivity period (typically 8-10 weeks) allows you to prepare financing, surveys and property searches with the confidence that you aren't wasting money on a sale that may fall through.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prospective purchasers are advised to inspect the property and commission an expert report where appropriate. Speak to one of our property specialists about our reservation deposit scheme to find out more.

Why you'll like it
An immaculate three-bedroom detached home on the sought-after Tascroft development in Warminster

This beautifully presented three-bedroom detached property is located within the popular Tascroft development on Osprey Road, Warminster, and offers stylish, modern living in truly turnkey condition.

Upon entering the property, the welcoming hallway provides access to a spacious living room positioned on the left-hand side. This room is a real standout feature, boasting a contemporary media wall and fireplace, creating a fantastic space to relax and unwind. To the opposite side of the hallway is a convenient downstairs cloakroom.

To the rear of the property is a superb open-plan kitchen/dining room, perfectly suited to modern family living and entertaining. The kitchen is well equipped with a central island, ample storage and worktop space, and double doors opening directly onto the rear garden, allowing plenty of natural light to flow through the room.

Upstairs, the property offers three well-proportioned bedrooms. The main bedroom benefits from fitted wardrobes and a modern en-suite shower room. The second bedroom is another generous double, while the third bedroom would comfortably suit a small double or spacious single bedroom, home office or nursery. A contemporary family bathroom completes the first floor.

Externally, the rear garden is a good size and designed with low maintenance in mind, being mainly laid to artificial lawn. There is also a decked seating area and a small patio, providing excellent spaces for outdoor dining and entertaining. A rear gate offers direct access to the driveway and garage, with the garage being a good size and ideal for parking or additional storage.

Immaculately presented throughout, this superb home is ready to move straight into and would make an ideal purchase for families, professionals or anyone seeking a high-quality modern home in a desirable Warminster location.

Early viewing is highly recommended.

 

Property location
Warminster offers a wide range of shopping and leisure facilities to include library, sports centre, swimming pool, schools, churches, doctors' and dentists' surgeries, hospital and post office. Warminster also benefits from a main line railway station to London Waterloo whilst the nearby A303 provides excellent road links to London to the east and Exeter to the west. Local attractions include Longleat House and Safari Park, Shearwater Lake, Stourhead and Salisbury Plain.

SUMMARY
Where contemporary and character combine, this two double bedroom property, has been extensively renovated throughout to create a wonderful home. There are charming features throughout including some beautiful fireplaces, as well as having a large rear garden, this home could be just right for you.


DESCRIPTION
Set in the village of Norton St Phillip, this property is a charming character cottage which has been extensively renovated, including a new roof, new electric central heating, as well as newly fitted kitchen and shower rooms.
The home has two double bedrooms, two shower rooms and a generous main reception room which has two fireplaces, one of which has a newly installed log burner.
The rear garden is a highlight to the home, offering a secluded space for private dining just off the rear lobby, as well as a large well landscaped rear garden. There is a large laid lawn, fringe bedding, large garden shed and space to the rear of the garden for a vegetable allotment.
Norton St Philip has a wonderful village community along side the other villages in the area, such as Hinton Charterhouse and Rode. The village is just 7 Miles from Bath City and 6 Miles to the vibrant town of Frome. This means you benefit from quaint countryside living but being very well connected by road or public transport into the stunning historic city of Bath.
Norton St Philip has a local store with post office facilities, nearby farm shop with cafe, two historic pubs with restaurants, a well regarded primary school as well as a strong community presence.

Ground Floor 

Living Room 21' 3" Max x 13' 9" Max ( 6.48m Max x 4.19m Max )
The warmth of this home welcomes you immediately as you step into the main reception room. The log burning fire grabs your attention as the focal feature of the room as well as the tasteful decoration, including the wooden beams on the ceiling. The room benefits from two windows to the front, stairs leading to the first floor and ample space for living and further dining space next to the second fireplace.

Kitchen  10' 10" x 7' 6" ( 3.30m x 2.29m )
Newly fitted, this modern designed kitchen is in keeping with the character of the property but offers a delightful modern feel. There are two windows to the rear offering views into the rear garden. The kitchen is fitted with a porcelain sink, dishwasher, induction hob with built-in extractor, cooker, integrated microwave and fridge freezer. Alongside this there is ample storage and work surface space for meal preparation.

Utility Room 4' 9" x 5' 9" ( 1.45m x 1.75m )
The rear lobby, is used for the utilities such as washing machine and tumble dryer, this has access into the rear garden and offers a good space to down tools before stepping back into the home.

Shower Room 6' 2" x 5' 5" ( 1.88m x 1.65m )
The ground floor shower room, has been newly fitted and offers a walk in shower, wash basin, w.c. and base unit for storage.

First Floor 

Bedroom One 14' Max x 11' 5" Max ( 4.27m Max x 3.48m Max )
The master bedroom is a generous double with built in alcove storage either side of the chimney breast. The room is duel aspect with a window to the front and to the rear offering views into the rear garden.

Bedroom Two 13' 9" Max x 8' Max ( 4.19m Max x 2.44m Max )
The second bedroom is another double room with a window front and space for a double bed, a fitted wardrobe and free standing storage.

Shower Room 3' 9" x 7' 2" ( 1.14m x 2.18m )
The first floor shower room, again newly fitted, has a walk in shower, was basin, w.c. and base unit for storage.

External  

Rear Garden  
The garden is a real highlight to this home, stepping from the rear lobby there is a stone courtyard with space for a bistro seating area, potted plants and storage. There are stone steps leading to the main garden area, mainly laid to lawn the garden offers a space of peace and tranquillity, some floral fringe beds give variety to the landscape and established flowers and shrubs will be easy to maintain. To the rear of the garden is a large shed and there is a space at the end of the garden for a vegetable plot for your own recreation.

HELP TO GET YOU MOVING EVENT

Join us for our Help to get you moving event on 28th & 29th March and discover a stress free way to move into your new home sooner than you think.

What’s available on the day?

FREE financial advice from our expert team
Guidance through the home buying process, and the range of buying schemes available to you
Complimentary coffee, tea and light refreshments as you explore the possibilities
We look forward to seeing you there!

We are now 80% sold! Book your appointment on today to come and explore our show homes, the 3 bedroom semi detached Lavender, and the 3 bedroom detached Whitebeam. Both of our show homes have been beautifully furnished with products sourced from local businesses, reflecting our commitment to supporting the community and investing in the areas where we build.

Located just 1 mile south of the historic town of Frome and surrounded by beautiful countryside, Keyford on The Green offers a selection of high quality 2, 3, and 4-bedroom homes. Whether you’re a first-time buyer, a growing family, or a professional seeking a spacious and contemporary home, Keyford on The Green has a new home for you.

Known for its vibrant culture, rich history, monthly markets, and independent shops and eateries, Frome provides the perfect base for relaxation and exploration of rural Wiltshire, Bath, and Somerset.

One of the standout features of Keyford on The Green is its fantastic location, and it’s connection to local cities and towns such as Warminster, Westbury, Midsomer Norton, Bath & Bristol. Frome train station is also less than 10 minutes away, offering quick and convenient train services to Bristol, Gloucester, London and beyond. This makes it an ideal location for those who need to commute for work, but prefer to enjoy a more peaceful suburban setting when at home.

At Keyford on The Green, we are committed to building homes that embrace sustainable living. All of our homes are designed with energy efficiency in mind, and we are proud to be the first site to incorporate Air Source Heat Pumps, which harness ambient air to generate warmth, making them both highly energy-efficient and environmentally friendly. Additionally, every home features Solar Panels and EV charging points , supporting the move toward a greener future.

We also take great pride in using Recon Stone in the construction of our traditionally built homes, a sustainable and durable material that enhances the aesthetic appeal while being environmentally responsible.

QUOTE: EB0191  Believed to date back to the 1800’s this property has undergone extensive refurbishment but maintains its character and charm at every turn. If you have been dreaming of owning a piece of history in a picturesque village, look no further than this enchanting end-terrace cottage in Maiden Bradley. 

Located in the conservation village where you will find a perfect blend of rural tranquillity and community spirit. This delightful cottage over two floors offers plenty of space for you to create the home of your dreams.

You will be delighted by the contemporary feel of this period home which is thoughtfully renovated throughout but retaining the authenticity of its origins. Having both a living room and family room there is plenty of living space, these rooms are adorned with wooden beams, and each have a focal point, fireplaces inset with woodburners, which create a warm and inviting atmosphere, this also adds not only warmth but also a touch of rustic charm to these spaces. Imagine curling up on a cold winter's night with an enjoyable book and a cup of tea, the crackling fire casting a soft glow around you.

The kitchen diner in this cottage is a true delight for anyone who loves to cook. Featuring sage green cabinetry that adds a pop of colour to the space, the kitchen is equipped with a modern oven and electric hob, making meal preparation a breeze. There are spaces for a washing machine and fridge and freezer. The dining area provides plenty of room for a large dining table where you can gather with family and friends for meals and celebrations.

The cloakroom is thoughtfully designed and includes a shower cubicle adding convenience for both residents and guests.

The storage cupboard under the stairs provides ample space to keep your belongings and small household appliances organised and out of sight ensuring a clutter free environment.

Upstairs, the cottage features two double bedrooms and a single, each offering a serene retreat for rest and relaxation. The main bedroom with open exposed beams comes complete with large built-in cupboard, providing sufficient storage space and a clutter free environment.

The second bedroom is light filled, ideal for a growing family or hosting guests, whilst the single bedroom could also be used as a spacious home office if needed.

The beautifully appointed family bathroom is a true sanctuary, featuring a luxurious roll-top freestanding bath perfect if you like a leisurely soak in the tub. There is also a heated towel rail keeping your towels dry for your next pamper session. So, with the convenience of a downstairs shower room and a family bathroom morning will never be a rush for busy households.

The garden is a true oasis, featuring a stunning patio area where you can relax and unwind, surrounded by lush shrubs and foliage that provide ample privacy. Imagine sipping your morning coffee in this serene setting or hosting intimate gatherings with loved ones in this enchanting outdoor space. 

In addition to the rear garden, this property also boasts a front garden which is established with fruit trees and shrubs, there is a historical well at the bottom of the garden too which is covered.

In conclusion, the cottage's historical charm and unique features make it a desirable property for those seeking a traditional home. Whether you are looking for a weekend retreat or a permanent residence, this property offers the perfect blend of old-world charm and contemporary living. Come and get your own piece of vintage.

Why choose Maiden Bradley?

Maiden Bradley is a picturesque village located in the beautiful countryside of Wiltshire. Known for its charming cottages and historic buildings, Maiden Bradley offers a tranquil setting for residents and visitors alike. The village's rich history and stunning landscapes make it a popular destination for those seeking a peaceful retreat in the English countryside. Living in Maiden Bradley offers a quaint countryside experience with the Bradley Hare Pub set within the Duchy of Somerset estate and the family run Bradley Pantry adding to the charm. The refurbished pub provides a cosy place to unwind and socialise, while the store offers locally sourced produce, and you can even sit down and enjoy coffee and fabulous homemade delights. There are also community gardens currently with around thirty members with new members welcomed allowing you to get involved in the growing of vegetables, flowers and soft fruit for the local residents and their families. The community garden also has hives and wildlife areas for maximum biodiversity. A village hall with regular events and a recreation ground. The Potting Shed, which describes itself as a private and secluded boutique beauty studio. These local establishments create a sense of community and make daily life more enjoyable for residents in Maiden Bradley to an already close-knit community.

Do not miss the opportunity to make this charming cottage your new home. Whether you are a first-time buyer, a growing family, or looking to downsize, this property ticks all the boxes. So, to delve deeper into the details of this fantastic offering, book your personal tour with Emma Butcher today where you will have the opportunity to see it for yourself and learn more about this lovely home and its potential.

The property also has the additional benefits of a complete rewire and new plumbing, soft touch light switches, electric storage heating, new supersonic immersion water tank, double-glazed windows, insulated loft space with light, on-street parking.

Kindly note some items mentioned or seen in the photographs may not be included in the property, please check with the Property Agent. For further information or details about this property please visit. emmabutcher.exp.uk.com

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
STAMP DUTY PAID BY SELLER
A unique opportunity to acquire a 3 double bed period semi-detached maisonette adjacent to the Green in the very heart of West Bay with rear driveway/parking and large private balcony commanding exceptional views

SITUATION: The property is located in the very heart of the coastal village of West Bay adjoining the far side of The Green which provides ambling/seating space for visitors. In front of this is the renowned motorbike enthusiasts' meeting area. The George pub is next door and there are kiosks around the edge, with the renowned Riverside Restaurant and access to the summer-long fairground close by.

The views are directly over the harbour, riverside and sea and the central area of West Bay with its Church, vintage centre, The Salt House with the Green in front providing for markets and exhibitions, the Discovery Centre and the beaches for leisure and swimming lies approx 3 mins away. The piers offer fishing and crabbing opportunities, the harbour offers fishing and touring boat trips, the river offers canoeing, there is a well supported Gig-rowing Club, a Sunday car-boot sale and there is a golf club on East Cliff. West Bay offers access to the Jurassic Coastline and the South West Coastal Paths. There is also a convenience store on the entrance to West Bay with laundrette.

There is also an active local community group providing many events which include a Raft Race, West Bay Days, Fireworks and Boxing Day Swim.

The vibrant town of Bridport lies some 2 miles to the north and is accessed by footpaths and by regular buses. Here there is a good variety of individual shops boosted by a twice-weekly street market, an artists' and vintage quadrant, an Art Centre, Electric Palace theatre/cinema, Leisure Centre with indoor swimming pool and central Square which hosts bands and festivals all year round.

THE PROPERTY comprises a maisonette apartment known as 16B above a commercial premises presently used as an amusement arcade. It is a period property originally part of The Customs House and has an impressive frontage. It benefits from a rear outside stepped access to the first floor private entrance door and owns the driveway and parking area to the north providing parking for 2 cars and also has a large private first floor balcony to the south providing a magnificent view point over the harbour, river, central space to the sea and to the cliffs.

This penthouse maisonette offers spacious and light 3 double bed accommodation with good head height and should appeal to a variety of buyers wanting to experience the central hub of West Bay with all its offerings.

There is a possibility of purchasing the freehold interest or a long leasehold arrangement with or without the commercial property below which would suit a variety of business interests. The guide price for the sale of the whole property freehold is £950,000.

DIRECTIONS: Entering West Bay from Bridport, turn right to the harbour and proceed into the centre and the property will be seen across The Green on the right-hand side.

THE MAISONETTE APARTMENT has a concrete stepped access off the rear parking area leading to a FIRST FLOOR ENTRANCE PORCH opening to a LANDING AREA with storage cupboard off and 3 steps leading up to an INNER HALL area with coat hooks and modern electric consumer unit. A door leads off from here into the:
LARGE LIVING ROOM which features a large attractive open fireplace with slated reveals and beam over and with plinth extending into the room. There are two understairs' cupboards built into the staircase which rises to the first floor. There are double doors opening onto the south balcony and double wooden doors opening into the:
LARGE CONSERVATORY which extends on the south side with double doors also opening to the:
BALCONY which has a paved floor and half-walled frontage providing an excellent family al-fresco dining area with superb views over the bay to be enjoyed.
KITCHEN/DINING ROOM with good dining table space and well fitted with a comprehensive range of wooden units including wall-mounted cupboards, base cupboards and drawers with work surfaces over and including a large gas cooker Range. Potterton wall-mounted gas boiler providing central heating and hot water and a tiled surface over the original staircase, There is a large fridge/freezer, a window to the north and a part-glazed door opening to the:
UTILITY which has a storage area and space and plumbing for a washing machine and door to the CLOAKROOM which is fitted with a toilet and basin.
BEDROOM 1 offers a double room with double, louvre-doored wardrobe fitted.
BEDROOM 2 offers a view to the east and built-in louvre-doored wardrobe,
BEDROOM 3 is a large double bedroom with window to the north and has a 4-doored wardrobe built in. There is also an airing cupboard with a new hot water cylinder and slatted shelves.
FAMILY BATHROOM with raised shower cubicle, panelled bath, pedestal basin and low level WC and a Velux window.

OUTSIDE
There is a gravelled driveway to the rear off George Street providing parking for at least a couple of cars and with right of way/parking for the adjoining Customs House.

SERVICES: Mains water, drainage, electricity and gas central heating with Potterton annually serviced boiler. Council Tax Band 'B'. EPC - D.

TENURE: There is a possibility of obtaining the freehold interest or a 999-year lease dependent on requirements.

CONTENTS: The property can be purchased with the contents if required.

https://www.rightmove.co.uk/properties/1.full_text:
Beautifully presented contemporary three bedroom village house with outstanding country views.

The Property - 9 Purbeck Close is all about its position and its views. The property occupies an elevated position above the village with views from many of the principal rooms taking the wonderful rolling Dorset countryside framed beautifully by its picture windows. In recent years the property has been substantially extended and refurbished in some style bringing it firmly into the 21st century. To cap it all the property sits to the centre of a pretty rural village that has retained its public house and lies within a short drive to Bridport town centre itself.

The living accommodation is both polished and flowing with a large square kitchen /dining room to the front of the property with the dining area to one side and a kitchen area to the other all laid to a limestone floor. The kitchen is fitted with a an island unit to its centre with Minerva work surfaces over. Integral appliances include an electric double oven one of which doubles is a microwave, an induction hub with a an extractor ham hood over and a dishwasher. There is space for an American style fridge freezer. Beyond the kitchen, the sitting room centres on a fireplace equipped with a woodburning stove that flows through to a garden room that has bifold doors to one side with both rooms laid to Karndean flooring while both the kitchen and the sitting room have double doors leading out onto the gardens. In addition downstairs there is good sized entrance hall with a contemporary shower room to one side equipped with a double sized shower.

Upstairs there are three good bedrooms, the principle of which has a walk in wardrobe, served by a family bathroom that is equipped with both a bath and a shower. The property has mains, drainage, oil fired central heating, a water softener, UPVC double glazing and is beautifully decorated throughout.

Outside - To the front of the property there is parking for a car in front of the garage that is equipped with an up and over door and useful storage area in the roof void. From the rear of the garage a wheelchair friendly ramp gives access to the rear garden. The gardens face South and West with four sitting areas of which two are laid to Indian sandstone. The gardens are mature and well stocked with a range of herbaceous and shrub plants all designed to give shape colour and form throughout the year.

Situation - Uploders is a particularly attractive, sought-after village, to the north east of the former rope-making town of Bridport. The village has a public house to its centre with numerous walks to be had from the village itself and the Jurassic coast just a short drive away at West Bay. Bridport itself is a thriving town with markets held on Wednesdays and Saturdays and has a wide variety of shops, public houses and restaurants. The county town of Dorchester lies a comfortable motoring distance away to the east providing with Bridport a range of cultural, recreational and shopping facilities. Sporting, walking and horse riding opportunities abound within the area, and there is a choice of primary and secondary schooling.
